title: CDB - Operator KPIs
description: Minimal operator KPI slice for issue #203. Postgres trades are the current canonical source for executed trades. Positive-trade count and positive-trade rate stay blocked until the active runtime path persists per-trade outcomes.

panels.title: TRADES MADE
panels.description: Canonical operator count from PostgreSQL trades over the selected Grafana time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*)::double precision AS "Trades" FROM trades WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp)

options.content:
Not shown as a number yet.

Current active runtime path persists executed trades in `trades`, but it does not persist a per-trade positive or negative outcome field. `positions.realized_pnl` is not a safe historical trade counter.

Blocked on the same contract gap.

This should stay a derived query, not a raw metric, once a reliable positive-trade numerator exists on the active runtime path.

panels.title: TRADES OVER TIME
panels.description: Executed trades grouped per hour from PostgreSQL trades over the selected Grafana time range.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(timestamp, '1h'), COUNT(*)::double precision AS "Trades" FROM trades WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content:
- `Trades made`: live from `trades.timestamp`
- `Positive trades`: not yet repo-backed on the active runtime path
- `Positive trades %`: intentionally withheld until the numerator exists

Issue anchor: `#203`.

This dashboard stays deliberately narrow and does not backfill unsupported KPI semantics from non-canonical paths.
